title: It Is Not Growing Like A Tree by Ben Jonson — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
It is not growing like a tree
In bulk doth make Man better be;
Or standing long an oak, three hundred year,
To fall a log at last, dry, bald, and sere:
A lily of a day
Is fairer far in May,
Although it fall and die that night—
It was the plant and flower of light.
In small proportions we just beauties see;
And in short measures life may perfect be.
meaning: "It Is Not Growing Like A Tree" by Ben Jonson is a nature poem consisting of 10 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "It is not growing like a tree In bulk doth make Man better be;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ben Jonson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
